bugs.title: Mail Search Doesn't Work
bugs.subtitle: The search bar is purely decorative
bugs.description: You need to find an email. You type in the sender's name. Nothing. You try the subject line. Nothing. You try a unique word you know was in the email. Nothing. You give up, open Gmail in a browser, search there, find it instantly. This has been broken for over a decade.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of Mail users experience search failures

behavioralFlow.description: The typical journey of a failed Mail search

steps.description: Type part of a name or subject
Maybe it needs the complete word?
Sender name, subject keywords, desperation sets in
Accept that Mail has failed you again
Navigate to gmail.com, maybe log in
Type the same search, find it instantly

powerUserTax.description: Extra time spent by users trying to fix the unfixable

sinks.description: Google how to do it, find the terminal command, run it, wait anxiously
Search 'Apple Mail search not working' and read forums
Remove and re-add accounts, toggle settings randomly
Download Spark, Airmail, Edison... give up and return to Mail

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Average urgency when searching for email

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Search indexing is a solved problem. Two engineers, one sprint.

bugs.title: Autocorrect Won't Take No For An Answer
bugs.subtitle: You fixed it. It unfixed it. You fixed it again. It unfixed it again.
bugs.description: You type a word. Autocorrect changes it. You delete it and type what you meant. Autocorrect changes it again. You fix it AGAIN. It changes it AGAIN. You've now manually corrected this word twice, clearly signaling you want it this way. Autocorrect doesn't care. It will die on this hill.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of users experience the re-correction loop regularly

steps.description: A name, technical term, slang, or just a valid word
Changed to something you didn't want
Delete and retype your original word
It saw you fix it. It doesn't care.
Surely NOW it will learn?
Sometimes it takes three attempts to stick
Re-read the message to make sure it didn't change again

powerUserTax.description: Attempts to teach autocorrect who's boss

sinks.description: Add the word to text replacements, which sometimes works
Nuclear option that loses all your learned words
Give up on the feature, suffer typos instead

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Depends on context and embarrassment potential
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual chat, 2.0 = autocorrected your boss's name wrong

engineeringEstimate.explanation: If user manually corrects the same word twice, stop 'correcting' it. Simple counter.

bugs.title: Apple Pay: Card Icon Changes Address
bugs.subtitle: The credit card icon doesn't change the credit card
bugs.description: You're checking out and need to change your card. You see a button with a credit card icon and your address. You tap it. It changes your address. Not the card. To change the card, you need the other button that says 'Change Payment'. The one without the card icon. Every. Single. Time.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of users who try to change cards tap the wrong button first

steps.action: Need to use a different card
steps.description: This purchase should go on a different card
There's a credit card icon! That must be it
Confidently tap the button with the credit card on it
Wait, this is for addresses? But there was a card icon!
Dismiss the address picker
Tap 'Change Payment' - the one WITHOUT the card icon
Select the correct card

powerUserTax.description: Learning doesn't help because the UX is backwards

sinks.description: You KNOW the card icon is wrong, but your brain still reaches for it
Watch someone else make the same mistake, explain the backwards logic

shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = browsing, 2.0 = line of people behind you

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Move the card icon to the payment button. Literally one icon swap.

bugs.title: Google Contacts Sync is a Black Hole
bugs.subtitle: It's syncing. Or is it? Nobody knows.
bugs.description: You add a contact on your Mac. Open your iPhone. It's not there. Check your Mac again. Still there. Check your iPhone in an hour. Maybe it's there now? You edit a contact on your phone. The change doesn't appear on your Mac. Or it does, but three days later. The Contacts app freezes for 30 seconds while 'syncing' in the background. There's no progress indicator. No status. Just faith.

scope.featureUsageLabel: of users sync Google contacts with Apple devices
scope.bugEncounterLabel: of Google contacts users experience sync issues

steps.description: Create a new contact or update an existing one
Open Contacts on your other Apple device
The change hasn't synced. Or has it?
Try to manually trigger a sync somehow
Contacts becomes unresponsive for 30+ seconds
After all that, the change still isn't visible
Hope it eventually syncs on its own

powerUserTax.description: Desperate attempts to make sync work

sinks.action: Toggle Google account off and on
sinks.description: Disable and re-enable the account in Settings
Nuclear option that sometimes helps temporarily
Verify if the contact is at least on Google's servers
Sync creates duplicates that need manual merging

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Contact info is needed when you need it
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual lookup, 2.0 = need to call someone urgently

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Add sync status indicator, fix the background sync, show progress. Three engineers, two sprints.

bugs.title: AirDrop: Looking for Devices...
bugs.subtitle: They're right next to each other. They can't see each other.
bugs.description: You want to send a photo to someone standing next to you. You open AirDrop. 'Looking for devices...' Their phone is in their hand. AirDrop is on. They're in your contacts. Nothing. You both toggle WiFi. Nothing. Toggle Bluetooth. Nothing. Turn AirDrop off and on. Sometimes it works. Usually you just text it instead.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of AirDrop attempts have discovery issues

steps.description: Select the thing you want to share
The spinner spins. And spins.
They're literally holding it. You can see their screen.
Is it set to Everyone? Contacts Only? Wait, try Everyone.
The universal troubleshooting ritual
Sometimes after all that, it finally shows up
The reliable fallback

sinks.action: Remind people to unlock their screen
sinks.description: AirDrop requires both screens to be on and unlocked
Having hotspot on breaks AirDrop. Not documented anywhere.

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Usually trying to share something in the moment
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual, 2.0 = trying to share a photo at an event

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Fix the discovery protocol, add manual IP entry fallback. Two engineers, two sprints.

bugs.title: iCloud Photos: 'Uploading X Items'
bugs.subtitle: X has been 847 for three weeks.
bugs.description: You took some photos. iCloud says 'Uploading 847 items'. You wait. Next day: 'Uploading 847 items'. Week later: 'Uploading 847 items'. Is it stuck? Is it working? Is there an error? There's no progress bar. No ETA. No way to know which items. Just a number that never changes. Faith-based cloud storage.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of users see stuck/mysterious upload status
scope.frequencyLabel: times checking upload status per day

steps.description: Important moments you want backed up
Open Photos, scroll to bottom, see the message
A number with no context
Maybe it changed? Probably not.
The number hasn't changed in days
Toggle iCloud off/on, restart phone, connect to WiFi

sinks.description: Log into web to verify photos actually uploaded
Try to understand if uploads are actually happening
Nuclear option that may cause duplicates

shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = random screenshots, 2.0 = only copy of important event

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Add a real progress bar, show which items, show errors. Two engineers, one sprint.

bugs.title: Spotlight: 'Indexing...'
bugs.subtitle: Apple really can't do search.
bugs.description: You press Cmd+Space to search. Nothing comes up. Or it's slow. You check: Spotlight is 'Indexing'. It's been 'Indexing' for two days. Your laptop isn't new. Nothing changed. It just decided to re-index everything. Meanwhile, search doesn't work. You can't find apps, files, or emails. The computer is slow because Spotlight is churning in the background.

scope.featureUsageLabel: of Mac users rely on Spotlight
scope.bugEncounterLabel: of users experience stuck indexing annually
scope.frequencyLabel: indexing issues per day (when it happens, it lasts days)

steps.description: Cmd+Space, type what you need
The thing you know exists doesn't appear
System Preferences > Spotlight, or click menu bar
Estimated time remaining: Unknown
It can take 1-3 days to finish
Use Finder like an animal

sinks.description: Add drive to Privacy list, remove it, wait
sudo mdutil -E / and hope for the best
Give up on Spotlight entirely

shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual file lookup, 2.0 = can't find critical document

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Fix incremental indexing, show real progress, don't re-index unnecessarily. Three engineers, two sprints.

bugs.title: Personal Hotspot Won't Auto-Connect
bugs.subtitle: Three attempts. Every time. When you need it most.
bugs.description: You're at a coffee shop with no WiFi. Your laptop says it will 'automatically' connect to your iPhone's hotspot. It tries. It fails. You click it again. 'Connection failed.' You turn hotspot off and on. Try again. Failed. You unlock your phone, open Settings, stare at the Hotspot screen to 'activate' it. Finally, third or fourth attempt, it connects. This happens every single time.

scope.featureUsageLabel: of users use Personal Hotspot regularly
scope.bugEncounterLabel: of hotspot connections require multiple attempts

steps.description: You're somewhere without WiFi, need to work

steps.action: Click iPhone hotspot in WiFi menu
steps.description: It's right there in the list
Spinner spins hopefully
No explanation. Just failed.
Click it again, same result
The secret ritual: staring at the hotspot screen 'activates' it
Now it works. Why? Nobody knows.

sinks.description: Leave phone with hotspot settings visible to maintain connection
Delete the hotspot from saved networks, add fresh

sinks.action: Give up and use USB tethering
sinks.description: Plug in cable, hope that works

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Need internet NOW to get work done
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual browsing, 2.0 = on a deadline away from real WiFi

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Fix the Bluetooth/WiFi handshake, make auto-connect actually automatic. Two engineers, one sprint.

bugs.title: macOS 26 Window Resizing Doesn't Work
bugs.subtitle: The corner is there. You just can't click it.
bugs.description: You try to resize a window by grabbing the corner. You miss. You try again. Miss. You hover precisely where the corner should be. Nothing. Turns out the resize target is a tiny 19×19 pixel zone, but thanks to Tahoe's huge corner radius, 75% of it is outside the visible window. You have to click in empty space next to the window to resize it. Intuitive. (All credit goes to to Norbert Heger at noheger.at for doing all of the extensive legwork on this one!

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of resize attempts miss on first try

steps.description: Need to make a window bigger or smaller
Move cursor to where the corner visually is
The window doesn't resize. You clicked inside the window.
Move cursor around trying to find the magic pixel
Finally grab it by clicking in empty space
Drag to desired size

powerUserTax.description: Workarounds for people who resize a lot

sinks.description: Use third-party tools like Rectangle for keyboard resizing
Give up on corners, only resize from edges

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Expand the resize hitbox to match visual expectations. One engineer, a few days.

bugs.title: Apple Watch Widgets Won't Let Go
bugs.subtitle: You deleted that appointment. Your Watch didn't get the memo.
bugs.description: You delete a calendar event on your iPhone. You check your Watch. The event is still there. You wait. Still there. You open the Calendar app on your Watch — gone. But the widget? It's committed to that 2pm meeting you cancelled three hours ago. Widgets update when they feel like it. Which is never.

scope.featureUsageLabel: of Apple Watch users use widgets
scope.bugEncounterLabel: of widget users see stale data regularly
scope.frequencyLabel: glances at stale widgets per day

steps.description: Cancel that meeting on your iPhone or Mac
Check your schedule on the widget
It's still there. Mocking you.
Maybe if I look away and look back...
The app shows correct data. Widget doesn't.
Learn to distrust everything on your wrist

sinks.description: Turn it off and on again, the nuclear option
Maybe a fresh widget will behave
Launch the app hoping the widget notices

shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = checking casually, 2.0 = checking if you're late

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Push updates to widgets when data changes. Two engineers, one sprint.

bugs.title: iOS Text Selection is Pure Chaos
bugs.subtitle: You just wanted to move the cursor. Now everything is selected.
bugs.description: You want to position the cursor at the end of a line. You tap. It selects the last word. You try to grab the handle — it doesn't respond and deselects. You tap again, now it selects the whole sentence. You tap blank space to deselect — nothing. You tap five more times. On the fifth, it selects all. You switch apps hoping the selection disappears. You tap and hold — sometimes text selects, sometimes a menu appears, sometimes nothing. Got a Magic Keyboard? Good luck — trackpad selection just doesn't work half the time, but touching the screen does. Eventually you select all, delete everything, and retype from scratch. Apple has had 17 years to figure out touch text selection. This is where they landed.

scope.featureUsageLabel: of users edit text on iOS
scope.bugEncounterLabel: of text editing sessions involve selection frustration

steps.description: Just need to add a word at the end
You tapped precisely where you wanted. It selected a word.
Your finger is clearly on it. It doesn't respond.
You moved it one word. It jumped three lines.
Tap blank space. Nothing. Tap again. Nothing. Again. It selects more.
You've now selected everything. You don't know how.
It's faster to start over than fight the selection

sinks.description: Sometimes works. Sometimes shows a menu. Sometimes nothing.
Hold spacebar to move cursor — if you know it exists
Trackpad selection randomly doesn't work. Touch the screen — works fine. Why?
Copy, paste, undo with gestures — which also randomly don't register

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Text editing is constant and urgent
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual note, 2.0 = fixing a typo before sending

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Consistent hit targets, predictable selection behavior, trackpad parity with touch. Three engineers, two sprints. But honestly, how is this not done already?

bugs.title: AirDrop Shuffles Targets Mid-Tap
bugs.subtitle: You aimed for Sarah. You hit Dave. Dave now has your meme.
bugs.description: You want to AirDrop something to Sarah. Her name appears. You reach for it. In the 300 milliseconds between your brain saying 'tap' and your finger hitting glass, another device appears and the list reorders. You've just sent your photo to Dave. Or worse, to 'iPhone' — could be anyone. The list loves to shuffle right as you commit. It's like Apple is running A/B tests on your social anxiety.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of AirDrop attempts hit the wrong target due to reorder

steps.description: Their name appears in the AirDrop list
Your finger commits to the target
A new device appears, everything moves
You've sent it to someone else
Who did that go to? Can I cancel?
Text them 'sorry wrong person' or hope they decline

powerUserTax.description: Paranoid behaviors developed to avoid mis-sends

sinks.description: Stare at the list for 5 seconds before tapping, just in case
Read it twice before committing

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Depends on what you're sending and who's around
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = a link, 2.0 = something you really don't want Dave to see

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Make AirDrop a two-step flow like Messages or WhatsApp: tap AirDrop icon first, then pick recipient on a dedicated screen. No more shuffling targets in the share sheet. One engineer, a few days.

bugs.title: AirPlay Menu Reorders at Click
bugs.subtitle: You clicked 'Conference Room TV'. You got 'Karen's MacBook'.
bugs.description: You're about to present. You click the AirPlay icon. You see 'Conference Room TV'. You click it. In the instant between mousedown and mouseup, 'Karen's MacBook' appeared at the top and everything shifted down. You're now mirroring to Karen. Karen sees your desktop. Karen sees your Slack. The whole conference room watches you fumble to disconnect while your private messages scroll by.

scope.featureUsageLabel: of Mac users use AirPlay to external displays
scope.bugEncounterLabel: of AirPlay attempts hit wrong target due to reorder

steps.description: Ready to present, looking professional
There it is, right where you expect
Mouse commits to the click
Someone's MacBook just showed up at the top
Your desktop is now on Karen's laptop
Find the menu, click disconnect, pray nothing embarrassing was visible
This time watching the list like a hawk

sinks.action: Close all personal apps before presenting
sinks.description: Quit Slack, Messages, Mail... just in case

sinks.action: Wait for AirPlay list to stabilize
sinks.description: Count to three before clicking anything
Give up on wireless, carry a dongle everywhere

shameFactors.pressureLabel: You're presenting to people. Maximum stakes.
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = home TV, 2.0 = board meeting with executives watching

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Freeze the list while the menu is open. Add new items at the bottom, not the top. One engineer, one day.

bugs.title: Shortcuts Keeps Asking If You're Sure
bugs.subtitle: You set up the automation. You are the automation. It still doesn't trust you.
bugs.description: You created a Shortcut. You set up an automation trigger. It worked great for a while. Then one day, Apple asks you to confirm you still want to run it. You tap yes. A week later, it asks again. You tap yes. It keeps asking, randomly, forever. You made this automation. On your device. For yourself. But Apple treats you like a suspicious stranger trying to hack your own phone.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of automation users get random re-confirmation prompts

steps.description: The thing you set up attempts to do the thing
'Do you want to run this shortcut?' Yes. Obviously. I made it.
Sigh and tap 'Run' for the hundredth time
Automation runs. Peace restored. Temporarily.
Days or weeks later, same prompt. No pattern. No logic.

powerUserTax.description: Workarounds that defeat the purpose of automation

sinks.description: Toggle it off. Again. It keeps coming back.
Maybe starting fresh will fix it? (It won't.)
Just do the thing manually. Automation was a lie.

shameFactors.pressureLabel: Automations are meant to run without you
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = convenience automation, 2.0 = time-sensitive automation that failed because you weren't there to tap 'yes'

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Once a user confirms an automation, remember it. Stop asking. One engineer, a few days. This is not hard.

bugs.title: Finder Forgets Window Sizes
bugs.subtitle: You resized it. Finder doesn't care.
bugs.description: You resize a Finder window. You set it to fill the screen, or position it just right. You close it. You open a new Finder window. It's some random size again. Small. Wrong position. You resize it again. Close it. Open a new one. Wrong again. Sometimes restart helps. Sometimes it doesn't. Finder stores window state somewhere, but it's clearly written on a napkin that blows away in the wind.

scope.bugEncounterLabel: of users experience window size not persisting
scope.frequencyLabel: new Finder windows opened per day

steps.description: Drag it to the size you want
Done with this folder
Need to look at something else
Small, wrong position, not what you set
Sigh and drag it back to where you want it

sinks.description: Hold Option while closing to 'save' size. Sometimes works.
Nuke com.apple.finder.plist and start fresh
Use Moom, Magnet, or Rectangle instead

shameFactors.pressureLabel: It's the file manager. You use it constantly.
shameFactors.pressureExplanation: 1.0 = casual browsing, 2.0 = managing files on a deadline

engineeringEstimate.explanation: Persist window state reliably. It's a key-value store. This was solved in the 1990s. One engineer, one week.
